title: eriosorus villosulus
intro: Eriosorus villosulus is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae, commonly known as the woolly woodrose or woolly thistle. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to treat digestive issues, such as constipation and diarrhea, due to its laxative effects. The plant's roots and leaves contain saponins, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing potential health benefits in managing conditions such as arthritis and skin infections. Eriosorus villosulus can be prepared for herbal use through infusions or decoctions of the roots and leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application.
